limits.notes: Local whisper.cpp transcription runs on the local machine rather than a hosted API, so provider-side upload and duration caps do not apply.

large-v3-turbo.description: Large v3 Turbo — fast with near-v3 accuracy

limits.notes: Local whisper.cpp transcription runs on the local machine rather than a hosted API, so provider-side upload and duration caps do not apply.

gcloud.description: Google Cloud Speech-to-Text v2 via gcloud CLI auth and direct REST requests

chirp_3.description: Chirp 3 synchronous transcription with diarization, automatic punctuation, and word timing

limits.notes: Google Cloud Speech-to-Text V2 synchronous Recognize requests are limited to 10 MB or 60 seconds for inline local audio. AutoShow uses the us-speech.googleapis.com regional endpoint with location us and relies on the existing split-and-merge path for larger inputs.

aws.description: Amazon Transcribe batch speech-to-text via AWS CLI

standard.description: Standard batch transcription with language identification and speaker labels

limits.notes: Amazon Transcribe batch jobs require S3 input in the same AWS Region as the transcription request. Current AWS quotas document a 2 GB maximum audio file size and a 14,400-second maximum audio file length for standard transcription.
Deepgram's current docs publish a 2 GB file cap and separately state no limits to audio length for batch transcription, with a 10-minute processing-time cap for Nova/Base/Enhanced requests.

openai/whisper-large-v3-turbo.description: OpenAI Whisper large v3 turbo via DeepInfra's OpenAI-compatible transcription API

limits.notes: DeepInfra publishes OpenAI-compatible speech-to-text routes and model pages for whisper-large-v3 and whisper-large-v3-turbo, but current public docs do not publish fixed multipart upload or duration caps for the OpenAI-compatible transcription endpoint. AutoShow therefore keeps v1 split policy metadata notes-only and relies on retry-with-split when the provider rejects an oversized payload.

openai/whisper-large-v3.description: OpenAI Whisper large v3 via DeepInfra's OpenAI-compatible transcription API

limits.notes: DeepInfra publishes OpenAI-compatible speech-to-text routes and model pages for whisper-large-v3 and whisper-large-v3-turbo, but current public docs do not publish fixed multipart upload or duration caps for the OpenAI-compatible transcription endpoint. AutoShow therefore keeps v1 split policy metadata notes-only and relies on retry-with-split when the provider rejects an oversized payload.

openai/whisper-large-v3.description: OpenAI Whisper large v3 via Together's OpenAI-compatible transcription API

limits.notes: Together documents local file uploads and public URLs for wav, mp3, m4a, webm, and flac. Public docs do not publish a precise hard upload cap in the transcription API reference, so AutoShow keeps split policy notes-only and relies on retry-with-split when the provider rejects an oversized payload.

whisper-v3-turbo.description: Fireworks Whisper v3 turbo via the audio turbo transcription API

limits.notes: Fireworks transcription docs publish a 1 GB max audio file size, supported mp3/flac/wav formats, and no audio-duration limit.

whisper-v3.description: Fireworks Whisper v3 via the audio production transcription API

limits.notes: Fireworks transcription docs publish a 1 GB max audio file size, supported mp3/flac/wav formats, and no audio-duration limit.

whisper-large-v3-turbo.description: Cloudflare Workers AI OpenAI Whisper large v3 turbo

limits.notes: Cloudflare model docs do not publish a clear file-size or duration cap for the Workers AI STT endpoint. AutoShow keeps split policy notes-only and relies on retry-with-split for provider size errors.
Cloudflare model docs do not publish a clear file-size or duration cap for the Workers AI STT endpoint. AutoShow keeps split policy notes-only and relies on retry-with-split for provider size errors.

deapi.description: deAPI hosted Whisper transcription with exact provider quote support

WhisperLargeV3.description: Whisper Large V3 via deAPI unified transcription endpoints

limits.notes: deAPI publishes conflicting current upload limits for audio transcription: the audiofile2txt endpoint doc lists 10 MB, while the global Limits & Quotas page lists 80 MB for audio uploads. AutoShow keeps deAPI v1 split policy note-only and relies on exact provider pricing plus retry-with-split when the upload path is rejected as too large. Supported passthrough URLs are documented for YouTube, X/Twitter, Twitch, Kick, TikTok, and X Spaces.

Groq documents a 25 MB free-tier file cap and a 100 MB dev-tier file cap. Direct multipart attachments remain capped at 25 MB, so the current CLI keeps effectiveBytes at 25 MB. No maximum audio duration is currently documented.

Groq documents a 25 MB free-tier file cap and a 100 MB dev-tier file cap. Direct multipart attachments remain capped at 25 MB, so the current CLI keeps effectiveBytes at 25 MB. No maximum audio duration is currently documented.

stt-async-v4.description: Async v4 — diarized asynchronous transcription with token timestamps

limits.notes: Soniox's Files API documents a maximum uploaded file size of 524288000 bytes. Async docs separately cap file duration at 300 minutes. The transcription API also accepts audio_url, but no explicit remote URL size cap is currently published.

standard.description: Batch standard accuracy — faster turnaround with lower cost

limits.notes: Current Speechmatics docs publish a 1 GB limit for multipart uploads and explicitly suggest fetch-by-URL for larger files. They do not publish a current remote URL size cap or a batch-audio duration cap.

enhanced.description: Batch enhanced accuracy — highest accuracy for file transcription

limits.notes: Current Speechmatics docs publish a 1 GB limit for multipart uploads and explicitly suggest fetch-by-URL for larger files. They do not publish a current remote URL size cap or a batch-audio duration cap.

machine.description: Reverb Transcription via Rev async REST API with diarization enabled by default

limits.notes: The 5 TB limit applies to dashboard or source_config submissions; current CLI uploads multipart media files directly.

low_cost.description: Reverb Turbo transcription via Rev async REST API with diarization enabled by default

limits.notes: The 5 TB limit applies to dashboard or source_config submissions; current CLI uploads multipart media files directly.

scribe_v2.description: Scribe v2 — 90+ languages, diarization, word-level timestamps

limits.notes: Standard mode is capped at 10 hours. ElevenLabs also documents a 1-hour cap for multichannel mode, which the current CLI does not use.

Mistral's /v1/audio/transcriptions endpoint supports file, file_id, and file_url inputs. Current docs publish an approximately 3-hour audio-length cap for Voxtral Mini Transcribe 2, and the Known limitations page lists a 500 MB maximum file size for audio transcription. AutoShow uses those documented limits directly.

universal-3-pro.description: Universal-3 Pro — 6 languages, advanced prompting, diarization

limits.notes: AssemblyAI allows larger remote URLs than upload API submissions. The current CLI uploads first, so effectiveBytes tracks the smaller upload cap.

default.description: Default pre-recorded STT — diarization-enabled async transcription

limits.notes: Direct YouTube links are limited to 120 minutes. Current CLI uploads files instead. Enterprise plans can raise the audio-length cap to 4 hours 15 minutes.

happyscribe.description: Happy Scribe hosted automatic speech-to-text via signed upload and orders

auto.description: Automatic Happy Scribe STT with fixed en-US language and diarization enabled by default

limits.notes: Happy Scribe documents a signed-upload plus orders workflow for automatic transcription, but current public Product API docs do not publish fixed upload-size or audio-duration caps for automatic STT. AutoShow keeps Happy Scribe v1 split policy note-only and relies on explicit --split or retry-with-split if the provider rejects an oversized input.

supadata.description: Supadata hosted transcript retrieval and AI transcript generation from public source URLs

auto.description: Auto mode — fetch a native transcript when available, otherwise fall back to AI generation

limits.notes: Supadata accepts public YouTube, TikTok, Instagram, X/Twitter, Facebook, and direct media/file URLs. Pricing is credit-based rather than USD-based: auto mode is approximately 1 credit when a native transcript exists, otherwise approximately 2 credits per minute when AI generation is required.

native.description: Native mode — only fetch an existing transcript from the source platform

limits.notes: Supadata native mode only fetches existing transcripts from public source URLs. Responses indicating transcript-unavailable still consume approximately 1 credit per request.

generate.description: Generate mode — always create a transcript with AI from the public source URL

limits.notes: Supadata generate mode accepts supported public source URLs and bills approximately 2 credits per minute. The provider uses async jobs for longer content and does not accept local file uploads through the AutoShow CLI.
OpenAI Audio API uploads are capped at 25 MB. Current public docs do not publish a fixed duration cap for this model.
OpenAI Audio API uploads are capped at 25 MB. Current public docs do not publish a fixed duration cap for this model.

gemini-3-flash-preview.description: Gemini 3 Flash Preview prompted audio transcription

limits.notes: Gemini inline audio requests are kept safely under the 20 MB inline request limit; larger inputs use the Files API. Audio input is tokenized at 32 tokens/second, and final billed cost also includes prompt and output tokens.

GLM ASR supports wav and mp3 uploads, with a 25 MB file cap and 30-second audio-duration cap. AutoShow pre-splits longer audio and retries with split transcription if the provider rejects an oversized payload.

subtitle-track.description: Manual or auto-generated English subtitle track via yt-dlp

limits.notes: This adapter reuses subtitle tracks fetched by yt-dlp instead of uploading audio to a hosted STT API, so provider-side file-size and duration request caps do not apply.
